data.title: Burlington Stores, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Burlington Stores, Inc., a leading off-price retailer offering a wide range of branded merchandise, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of Fiscal 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market position.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $2,705.1 million, increased by 9.7% compared to the same period last year, driven by net sales from 81 net new stores and a 5% increase in comparable store sales.

Cost of Sales: $1,519.6 million, representing 56.3% of net sales, down from 57.2% in the prior year, due to increased merchandise margin and decreased freight costs.

Selling, General and Administrative Expenses: $949.9 million, representing 35.2% of net sales, slightly up from 35.1% in the prior year, primarily due to increased incentive compensation.

Net Income: $94.2 million, up from $73.8 million in the prior year, driven by higher sales and increased gross margin rate.

Net Income Per Common Share - Basic: $1.49, compared to $1.16 in the prior year.

Net Income Per Common Share - Diluted: $1.47, compared to $1.15 in the prior year.

Business Highlights

Store Expansion: During the six-month period ended August 2, 2025, Burlington Stores, Inc. opened 44 new stores, including eight relocations, and permanently closed six stores, resulting in a total of 1,138 stores across 46 states, Washington D.C., and Puerto Rico.

Product Offering Diversification: The company continues to offer a wide range of in-season, high-quality branded merchandise at up to 60% off other retailers’ prices, including women’s apparel, menswear, youth apparel, baby, beauty, footwear, accessories, home, toys, gifts, and coats.

Inventory Management: Inventory levels increased to $1,414.8 million as of August 2, 2025, primarily due to an increase in reserve inventory and the addition of 81 net new stores since the end of the second quarter of Fiscal 2024. Reserve inventory accounted for 50% of total inventory, up from 41% in the previous year.

Operational Initiatives: Burlington Stores is focusing on driving comparable store sales growth by more effectively chasing sales trends, operating with leaner inventories, investing in merchandising capabilities, and enhancing existing categories while introducing new ones.

Future Store Growth: The company plans to expand its retail store base with a goal of operating 2,000 stores over the long term, averaging about 100 net new stores per year from Fiscal 2024 through Fiscal 2028.

Operational Efficiency: Initiatives to enhance operating margins include improving operational flexibility, optimizing markdowns, and optimizing the supply chain through transportation initiatives and investments in automation.

Seasonal and Economic Challenges: The company acknowledges uncertainties such as macroeconomic conditions, inflationary pressures, and consumer spending habits, which could impact revenues and profitability.

Geographical Performance: All revenue is derived from operations within the United States, and the company manages its business activities on a consolidated basis.

Future Outlook: Burlington Stores is committed to enhancing the store experience through relocations and downsizes, improving competitive positioning, and reducing occupancy costs. The company also plans to continue challenging expenses to drive operating leverage.

SEC Filing: Burlington Stores, Inc. [ BURL ] - 10-Q - Aug. 28, 2025

Liberty Energy Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >LBRT
data.description:
Liberty Energy Inc. (LBRT) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on August 28, 2025.

On August 26, 2025, Audrey Robertson, a member of the Board of Directors (the "Board") of Liberty Energy Inc., resigned from the Board effective immediately.

In February 2025, Ms. Robertson provided a conditional resignation from the Board due to her nomination as the Assistant Secretary of Energy for Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy at the Department of Energy of the United States ("DOE"). This prior resignation would have been effective upon her confirmation to this position by the United States Senate. Since that time, Ms. Robertson has decided to begin work at DOE and contemporaneously resign from the Board while the confirmation process is pending, and this current notice of resignation from the Board supersedes the one provided in February 2025.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1694028/000169402825000105/lbrt-20250826.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1694028/000169402825000105/0001694028-25-000105-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Brazilian stocks boost Latam stocks to over one-year peak, currencies edge higher
data.description:
Key points:
  • Latam stocks up 1.8%, FX up 0.7%
  • Mexico July seasonally-adjusted jobless rate at 2.6%
  • Brazil targets billion-dollar organized crime schemes in fuel sector

Brazilian stocks led gains in Latin America on Thursday, boosting a gauge tracking the region's equities to an over one-year high, while currencies edged higher as investors ramped up bets for a Federal Reserve rate cut next month.

Stocks in Brazil's benchmark Bovespa index hit a record high, last up 1.6%.

Shares of fuel distributors jumped after Brazilian authorities targeted billion-dollar organized crime schemes in the sector.

The real , hit its highest in two weeks and was last up 0.2%, as investors parsed through data that showed the General Market Price Index rose 0.36% in August, above expectations of a 0.21% rise.

Brazil's central government reported a primary budget deficit of 59.12 billion reais ($10.9 billion) in July, Treasury data showed, more than six times the deficit from the year-earlier period but close to analysts' estimates.

Mexican stocks hit an over three-month high, last up 1.1%, while the peso inched 0.1% higher. The country's seasonally-adjusted jobless rate stood at 2.6% in July, official data showed.

Bank of America analysts warned of weak growth, remittances and a deeper-than-expected easing by Banxico potentially weighing on the peso.

Mexican Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard said that his country is not pursuing a free trade agreement with Brazil but is instead exploring complementary agreements.

On a larger front, the MSCI gauge for Latin American stocks (.MILA00000PUS) jumped 1.8%, reaching its highest level since May 2024, while a similar gauge for currencies (.MILA00000CUS) rose 0.7%.

Traders doubled down on Fed rate-cut bets for next month after New York Fed President John Williams signaled that a cut was possible. The dollar edged lower, boosting most emerging market currencies globally.

A potential Fed rate cut in September could revive the dollar's role as a funding currency for carry trades in countries with historically high interest rates, such as Brazil.

"Even if the Brazilian central bank starts easing policy later this year, carry will still remain attractive. Even more so if the Fed decides to cut in September," analysts at BofA global research said.

There has been an increase in carry positions in Brazil for some time now and recent data has shown some signs of moderation in inflation, Alejandro Cuadrado, global head of FX and Latam strategy at BBVA, said, adding, this moderation "with such high nominal rates" provides a very strong nominal and real return.

Argentine stocks attempted to recover this week's losses, climbing 1.9% and set for their best day in over three weeks, while the peso advanced 1.3%.

Argentine President Javier Milei was rushed away by security on Wednesday after protesters hurled rocks and debris at his convoy during a public appearance in Buenos Aires province, Reuters witnesses said.

The Chilean peso rose 0.1%, while stocks in Santiago edged up 0.2%.

Peruvian equities gained 0.7%. The Ministry of Economy expects that Peru's economy would grow by an average of 3.2% between 2026 and 2029.

data.title: NIKE, Inc. Announces First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Earnings and Conference Call
data.description:
BEAVERTON, Ore.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--August 28, 2025--

NIKE, Inc. plans to release its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results on Tuesday, September 30, 2025, at approximately 1:15 p.m. PT, following the close of regular stock market trading hours. Following the news release, NIKE, Inc. management will host a conference call beginning at 2:00 p.m. PT to review results.

The conference call will be broadcast live over the Internet and can be accessed at https://investors.nike.com/. For those unable to listen to the live broadcast, an archived version will be available at the same location through 9:00 p.m. PT, October 21, 2025.

About NIKE, Inc.

NIKE, Inc., headquartered in Beaverton, Oregon, is the world's leading designer, marketer and distributor of authentic athletic footwear, apparel, equipment and accessories for a wide variety of sports and fitness activities. Converse, a wholly-owned NIKE, Inc. subsidiary brand, designs, markets and distributes athletic lifestyle footwear, apparel and accessories. For more information, NIKE, Inc.'s earnings releases and other financial information are available on the Internet at https://investors.nike.com/. Individuals can also visit https://about.nike.com/ and follow NIKE on LinkedIn, Instagram and YouTube.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250828728146/en/

CONTACT: Investor Contact:

Paul Trussell

investor.relations@nike.com

Media Contact:

Jay Paavonpera

media.relations@nike.com

data.title: Affirm Holdings Sees 1Q Rev $855M-$885M >AFRM
IREN Ltd SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
IREN Ltd, a company specializing in Bitcoin mining and AI Cloud Services, has released its annual Form 10-K report, showcasing substantial financial growth, strategic initiatives, and addressing key challenges and risks. The report highlights the company's efforts to expand its revenue streams and improve operational efficiency while navigating a complex regulatory and market environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $501.0 million, reflecting a significant increase from $187.2 million in the previous year, driven by higher Bitcoin mining revenue and the expansion of AI Cloud Services.

Operating Income: $17.3 million, compared to an operating loss of $27.2 million in the prior year, indicating improved operational efficiency and cost management.

Net Income: $86.9 million, a substantial turnaround from a net loss of $28.9 million in the previous year, primarily due to increased Bitcoin mining revenue and favorable changes in the fair value of financial instruments.

Basic Net Income Per Share of Ordinary shares: $0.74, reflecting the company's improved profitability.

Diluted Net Income Per Share of Ordinary shares: $0.66, highlighting the impact of potential share dilution on earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company generates revenue from two primary segments: Bitcoin mining and AI Cloud Services. Bitcoin mining revenue is derived from earning Bitcoin through block rewards and transaction fees, while AI Cloud Services revenue comes from providing access to cloud-based GPU computing for AI training and inference workloads.

Geographical Performance: The company operates data centers in the United States and Canada, with 83% of non-current assets located in the U.S. and 17% in Canada. The U.S. operations are primarily in Texas, while Canadian operations are in British Columbia.

Sales Units: As of June 30, 2025, the company had approximately 1.9k NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPUs deployed for AI Cloud Services, with plans to expand to approximately 10.9k NVIDIA GPUs by the end of 2025.

New Product Launches: In 2024, the company launched AI Cloud Services, which provides cloud-based GPU computing for AI workloads.

New Production Launches: The company is expanding its data center capacity with a direct-to-chip liquid cooling deployment at the Childress site in Texas, targeting energization by the end of 2025. Additionally, the Sweetwater 1 and Sweetwater 2 sites in Texas are under development, with grid connections expected in 2026 and 2027, respectively.

Future Outlook: The company is focused on expanding and diversifying its revenue streams into HPC and AI services, leveraging existing infrastructure to support growth in AI Cloud Services. It is also exploring opportunities to own, develop, and operate renewable generation and energy storage to lower power costs and generate additional revenue.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company is pursuing a strategy to expand and diversify its revenue streams into new markets, focusing on HPC and AI services. This includes the development of purpose-built AI data centers and the procurement of NVIDIA GPUs to enhance AI Cloud Services. The company is also advancing the design of direct-to-chip liquid cooling systems to support increased IT load at its Childress site.

Capital Management: The company issued $440 million of 2030 Convertible Notes and $550 million of 2029 Convertible Notes, with the proceeds used to support capital expenditures and liquidity needs. It also raised $635.1 million through the issuance of Ordinary shares under an At Market Sales Agreement. Equipment leasing agreements were entered into to finance the acquisition of NVIDIA GPUs, with structured lease payments and options for purchase at the end of the lease terms.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue monitoring funding markets for opportunities to raise additional capital to support its growth plans. It aims to leverage its existing infrastructure to expand its HPC and AI services, targeting a range of customers across various sectors. The company also intends to maintain its focus on securing low-cost, renewable energy sources to support its operations.

Challenges and Risks

Operational Risks: The company has a history of operating losses and may incur net losses in the future despite recent profitability. The rapid growth and evolving business model, including expansion into HPC and AI services, present challenges such as increased complexity, strain on resources, and potential under-capitalization.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to fluctuations in the digital asset market, including Bitcoin price volatility, which directly impacts its mining operations. The expansion into HPC and AI services introduces additional market risks due to rapid technological advancements and increasing competition.

Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to evolving regulatory environments, particularly concerning energy consumption and environmental impact. Recent regulatory actions in British Columbia and New York highlight potential restrictions on electricity supply for cryptocurrency mining, which could adversely affect operations.

Supply Chain Risks: The company relies heavily on a limited number of equipment suppliers, particularly for Bitcoin mining and HPC/AI services. Supply chain disruptions, including tariffs and geopolitical tensions, could impact equipment availability and costs.

Financial Risks: The company has significant indebtedness, which could limit cash flow and flexibility. The need for additional capital to finance growth and operations presents a risk, especially if market conditions are unfavorable.

Infrastructure Risks: The development of new facilities and expansion plans are subject to risks such as construction delays, increased costs, and regulatory hurdles. The reliance on electricity and internet connectivity poses additional risks, as outages could materially impact operations.

Management is actively addressing these challenges by diversifying revenue sources, enhancing operational efficiency, and exploring strategic partnerships. However, the success of these strategies is uncertain and subject to external factors beyond the company's control.

SEC Filing: IREN Ltd [ IREN ] - 10-K - Aug. 28, 2025

data.title: BILL Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
BILL Holdings, Inc., a leading provider of financial operations software for small and midsize businesses (SMBs), has released its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year ending June 30, 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth, strategic initiatives, and the company's ongoing efforts to enhance its product offerings and expand its market presence.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $1,462.6 million, an increase of 13% from fiscal 2024, primarily driven by a $163.1 million increase in transaction fee revenue due to higher total payment volume.

Gross Profit: $1,190.5 million, representing a gross margin of 81.4%, slightly down from 81.8% in fiscal 2024 due to changes in the cost mix associated with processing payments.

Operating Loss: $80.6 million, a 54% improvement compared to fiscal 2024, reflecting better cost management and increased revenue.

Net Income: $23.8 million, a significant turnaround from a net loss of $28.9 million in fiscal 2024, driven by increased revenue and effective cost control.

Net Income Per Share: Basic $0.23, Diluted $(0.07), indicating the company's return to profitability and improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company generates revenue primarily from subscription and transaction fees, with transaction fees contributing significantly due to increased total payment volume driven by customer adoption of products.

Sales Units: As of June 30, 2025, approximately 493,800 businesses used the company's solutions, processing approximately $330 billion in Total Payment Volume (TPV) during fiscal 2025.

Sales Units: The total number of transactions processed was 121.3 million, reflecting a 17% increase from the previous year.

New Product Launches: The company offers BILL Spend and Expense, a spend and expense management product that provides businesses with smart corporate cards and budget management tools.

New Product Launches: The company introduced a new offering, Supplier Payments Plus, enabling large suppliers to efficiently process and reconcile high volumes of payments from SMBs.

Future Outlook: The company expects the market for SMB back-office financial software and business-to-business payment solutions to continue evolving and growing, positioning itself to help businesses digitize their back offices.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue investing in research and development to enhance product functionality and customer experience, focusing on AI tools and offerings.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates further growth in its network, driven by customer and partner additions, and expects to continue scaling its business operations.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company issued $1.4 billion in aggregate principal amount of 0% convertible senior notes due 2030 to strengthen its capital structure and repurchased portions of its 2025 and 2027 Notes to manage debt maturity profiles. Additionally, the company focused on expanding its financial operations platform for small and midsize businesses, enhancing its product offerings, and increasing customer adoption.

Capital Management: The company executed a share repurchase program, buying back $236.4 million worth of shares under the August 2024 Share Repurchase Program and completed the repurchase of shares under this program in July 2025. It also repurchased $200 million of shares in connection with the issuance of the 2030 Notes. The company maintains a strong liquidity position with $1.0 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $1.2 billion in short-term investments. It has a total borrowing capacity of $600 million from its Revolving Credit Facilities, with $180 million drawn from the 2021 Credit Facility.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its share repurchase activities with a new $300 million share repurchase program authorized in August 2025. It aims to maintain a strong liquidity position to support future operational expenses and investments. The company is also exploring additional capital raising opportunities through equity or debt financing to fund future operations and obligations, including the repayment of outstanding convertible senior notes.

Challenges and Risks

Operating Losses and Profitability: The company has a history of operating losses and may not sustain or expand profitability in the future. Despite recent revenue growth and achieving profitability in certain quarters, there is uncertainty about maintaining this trend due to expected increases in costs and expenses.

Economic Dependence on SMBs: A significant portion of revenue comes from small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), which are more vulnerable to economic downturns. Volatile economic conditions in the U.S. and globally could adversely affect the company's business and operating results.

Customer Acquisition and Retention: The company's growth depends on attracting new customers and retaining existing ones. Challenges include converting trial customers to paying customers and promoting charge card usage. Failure in these areas could adversely affect revenue growth.

Credit Risk: The BILL Divvy Card offering exposes the company to credit risk related to spending businesses' ability to pay balances. Other offerings, such as invoice financing, also subject the company to credit risk.

Fraud Risk: The company may not effectively prevent fraudulent activities by customers or third parties, which could lead to financial losses and harm the business.

Competitive Market: The markets in which the company operates are highly competitive. Failure to compete effectively could harm operating results.

Regulatory Compliance: The company is subject to numerous regulations related to payments and financial services. Non-compliance could materially harm the business.

AI Integration: The use of artificial intelligence presents challenges, including potential reputational harm, competitive harm, and legal liability if not managed properly.

Partnership Dependencies: The company's business depends on relationships with accounting firms and financial institutions. Any disruption in these relationships could impact growth prospects.

Funding and Credit Facilities: The company relies on various funding sources to support its BILL Divvy Card offering. Changes in these arrangements could adversely affect business operations.

Interest Rate Fluctuations: Revenue from interest earned on customer funds is subject to changes in interest rates, which could impact financial results.

Foreign Exchange Risk: The company handles cross-border payments, exposing it to currency exchange rate fluctuations that may affect revenues and expenses.

Interchange Fee Variability: Revenue from interchange fees is subject to changes in card network rates and regulatory scrutiny, which could impact income.

SEC Filing: BILL Holdings, Inc. [ BILL ] - 10-K - Aug. 28, 2025

data.title: ACCURAY INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Accuray Incorporated, a leading radiation therapy company, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The report highlights the company's commitment to innovation in radiation treatments, strategic initiatives to expand its market presence, and the challenges it faces in the current economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenue: $458.5 million, increased by 3% compared to the previous year, driven by higher volume of shipments in China and APAC regions.

Gross Profit: $146.967 million, representing a 3% increase from the prior year, with a gross profit margin of 32.1%.

Income from Operations: $7.839 million, a significant improvement from $0.504 million in the previous year, indicating better operational efficiency.

Net Income: $(1.591) million, a reduction in net loss compared to $(15.545) million in the previous year, reflecting improved financial performance.

Net Loss Per Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.02), compared to $(0.16) in the previous year, showing a reduction in loss per share.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Accuray Incorporated is a radiation therapy company that develops, manufactures, sells, and supports innovative solutions designed to deliver precise radiation treatments for complex and common cases, enhancing patient care throughout the cancer treatment journey.

Product Portfolio: The company's key platforms include the CyberKnife and TomoTherapy systems, with the Radixact System as the next-generation TomoTherapy platform. These systems are designed to deliver advanced treatments such as stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS), stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT), and intensity modulated radiation therapy (IMRT).

New Product Launches: Accuray introduced the Accuray Helix, a CT-guided helical radiotherapy system, to address gaps in patient access to radiation medicine by combining affordability with automation and enhanced planning and delivery tools.

Geographical Performance: Accuray is expanding its sales and distribution capabilities internationally, with regional offices in Switzerland, China, and Japan, and direct sales staff in Western Europe, Japan, India, and Canada. The company is focusing on increasing its presence in promising international markets.

Strategic Partnerships: The company has formed several strategic partnerships and joint ventures, including collaborations with RaySearch Laboratories, Brainlab, C-RAD, and GE Healthcare, to enhance its product offerings and expand its market reach.

Manufacturing Operations: Accuray manufactures its CyberKnife and TomoTherapy platforms in Madison, Wisconsin, and the linear accelerator components in Chengdu, China. The company employs state-of-the-art manufacturing techniques and lean manufacturing practices to improve efficiency.

Research and Development: Accuray is committed to innovation, focusing on expanding clinical applications, product differentiation, and improving usability and performance. The company collaborates with research institutions and industrial partners to accelerate innovation and bring complementary products to market.

Regulatory Compliance: The company complies with various international regulations, including FDA requirements in the U.S., CE marking in Europe, and regulatory approvals in Japan and China, ensuring its products meet safety and efficacy standards.

Future Outlook: Accuray aims to increase physician adoption and patient awareness, expand the radiosurgery market, innovate through clinical development and collaboration, and grow its international sales to improve patient access to advanced radiation therapy solutions.

Strategic Initiatives

Joint Venture with CNNC High Energy Equipment: The company has entered into a joint venture with CNNC High Energy Equipment to manufacture and sell radiation oncology systems in China, aiming to capitalize on the growth potential in the Chinese market.

Capital Management: The company executed a significant refinancing strategy by entering into a new senior secured credit agreement, which includes $150 million in new term loan facilities, a $20 million delayed draw term loan facility, and a $20 million revolving credit facility. This refinancing was used to repay existing senior secured indebtedness and fund a cash payment as part of an exchange of a portion of the company's convertible notes. The company also issued detachable warrants to lenders as part of this refinancing effort. Additionally, the company has been actively managing its equity by issuing shares to settle convertible notes and implementing cost-saving measures to improve liquidity.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued challenges from inflation and supply chain disruptions, which are expected to impact gross margins and cash flows through at least calendar year 2025. The company plans to focus on maintaining sufficient cash resources and anticipated cash flows to fund operations for the next 12 months, while also exploring opportunities to refinance existing debt and manage capital expenditures strategically. The company is also evaluating its real estate needs and operational efficiencies to adapt to the uncertain macroeconomic environment.

Challenges and Risks

Economic and Geopolitical Risks: The company faces significant risks due to the current global economic environment, including inflation, recession, and currency fluctuations, which could adversely affect its business, financial condition, and results of operations. The uncertainty surrounding international tariffs, particularly between the United States and China, poses a risk of increased costs and decreased sales volume in China. Additionally, the company is exposed to risks from geopolitical conflicts, such as the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which could impact global economic conditions and financial markets.

Operational Risks: The company relies on single-source suppliers for critical components, which could harm its ability to meet product demand in a timely and cost-effective manner. The company also faces challenges in managing its growth, which requires significant investments in infrastructure, personnel, and systems. Furthermore, the company is subject to intense competition and rapid technological change, which may render its products obsolete if it fails to innovate and meet customer demands.

Regulatory Risks: The company must comply with various federal, state, and foreign laws and regulations. Changes in healthcare, trade, fiscal, and tax policies, particularly in the U.S., could impact the company's operations. The company also faces risks related to privacy, cybersecurity, and data protection laws, which are becoming increasingly stringent and complex globally.

Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to maintain or increase gross margins as a critical challenge, given the rising costs of materials and logistics due to inflation and supply chain disruptions. The company is focused on managing these costs and exploring ways to pass them on to customers where possible. Additionally, management is addressing the risks associated with its substantial indebtedness by ensuring compliance with financial covenants and exploring refinancing options if necessary.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, particularly foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could impact its financial results. The strengthening of the U.S. Dollar could make the company's products less competitive internationally, and the company may face challenges in raising local prices to offset currency impacts. The company is also monitoring interest rate risks associated with its credit facilities, as changes in interest rates could affect its annual interest expenses.

SEC Filing: ACCURAY INC [ ARAY ] - 10-K - Aug. 28, 2025

data.title: Dell Beat Earnings Estimates. Why the Stock Is Down. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

HP Inc.'s stronger-than-expected demand for personal computers could be a good omen for Dell Technologies, which reports earnings after the stock market closes Thursday.

HP reported financial results for its fiscal third-quarter after the market close on Wednesday. Earnings for the quarter were in-line with analyst estimates, while revenue came in above expectations.

"Despite fears of a PC demand slow-down owing to a tariff related pull-in last quarter, HP reported solid [revenue]," UBS analyst David Vogt wrote in a note. He raised his price target on HP to $29 from $26 and maintained a Neutral rating on the stock.

Shares of HP rose 2.8% to $27.87 on Thursday.

President Donald Trump has implemented tariffs in what he says is a bid to bring manufacturing back to the U.S. However, some tech hardware is exempt from the highest tariffs as Section 232 investigations into electronic products continue. Those investigations allow the U.S. government to look into whether imports of certain products are a threat to national security.

Even though HP PCs aren't affected by tariffs, there's a concern that consumers' purchasing power will suffer from the general increase of goods prices from higher import taxes. But so far, that hasn't happened yet.

HP CEO Enrique Lores told Barron's on Wednesday that HP believes the back-to-school season is "starting strong," which helped demand.

Also, Microsoft's Windows 10 operating system ends in October. When that happens, technical assistance and software updates will no longer be provided on Windows 10 systems, unless customers pay per device. Not only has this helped incentivize purchases of new PCs, but HP says that only about 50% of the installed base has been converted to Windows 11, leaving more room for upgrades in the months ahead.

This could all be a good sign for PC maker Dell.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect Dell to report second-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.29 a share on revenue of $29 billion.

Dell reports revenue for both its Client Solutions Group, which includes PCs and other commercial hardware, and its Infrastructure Solutions Group, which includes servers that help power artificial intelligence.

Analysts expect Dell to report revenue of $12.87 billion for its Client Solutions Group, which would be a 3.7% increase from the same period last year.

Wall Street will also be paying close attention to Dell's server demand for the quarter, as artificial-intelligence infrastructure investments remain a key focus for investors. Analysts expect Infrastructure Solutions Group revenue of $15.64 billion, a 34% jump from last year.

"Our supply chain checks suggest that AI server order momentum remains strong," BofA Securities analyst Wamsi Mohan wrote in a note on Aug. 15. He forecasts AI server revenue of $20.7 billion in the fiscal year 2026, above Dell's current guidance of more than $15 billion, he added.

He rates Dell as a Buy with a $165 price target.

Shares of Dell were up 0.5% to $133.12 Thursday. The stock has gained 16% this year.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: S&P 500, Dow score record high closes as Nvidia results buttress AI rally
data.description:
Key points:
  • Weekly jobless claims fall; corporate profits rebound in Q2
  • Snowflake surges on AI-driven revenue forecast
  • HP Inc gains on upbeat Q3 revenue
  • S&P 500 +0.32%, Nasdaq +0.53%, Dow +0.16%

The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average notched record high closes on Thursday after Nvidia's quarterly report fell short of investors' high expectations but confirmed that spending related to artificial intelligence infrastructure remains strong.

Shares of Nvidia dipped 0.8% after Sino-U.S. trade uncertainties prompted the leading AI chip designer to exclude potential China sales from its quarterly forecast late on Wednesday.

Investors viewed Nvidia's report, including a 56% surge in quarterly revenue, as confirmation that demand related to AI technology remains strong, supporting a rally in AI-related stocks that has propelled Wall Street to record highs in recent years.

Other AI heavyweights gained, with Alphabet adding 2%, Amazon up 1% and chipmaker Broadcom rising almost 3%.

"Nvidia is such an outlier that to say it was a disappointing print is only against the bar of borderline impossible expectations," said Ross Mayfield, an investment strategy analyst at Baird. "It's clear that the primary structural driver of this market, which is AI, is not going anywhere or cooling down."

The S&P 500 climbed 0.32% to end the session at 6,501.86 points, reaching a record high close for a second straight day.

The Nasdaq gained 0.53% to 21,705.16 points, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.16% to 45,636.90 points, exceeding its previous record high close on August 22.

Seven of the 11 S&P 500 sector indexes rose, led by communication services , up 0.94%, followed by a 0.68% gain in energy .

Nike slid 0.2% after the sports apparel seller said it was cutting less than 1% of its corporate workforce as it struggles to reclaim market share lost to rivals.

Reducing worries of a slowing economy, weekly jobless claims were lower than expected, while a separate report showed corporate profits rebounded in the second quarter.

Expectations that the Federal Reserve will soon cut interest rates to shore up economic growth have contributed to Wall Street's recent gains.

Investors on Friday will focus on Personal Consumption Expenditures data. Any signs of inflation increasing could temper broad expectations for easing at the Fed's policy meeting in September.

Traders are pricing in more than an 80% chance of an interest rate cut next month, according to CME Group's FedWatch.

On Thursday, Fed Governor Lisa Cook filed a lawsuit challenging U.S. President Donald Trump's attempt to remove her from office earlier this week.

Data analytics company Snowflake surged 20% after raising its forecast for fiscal 2026 product revenue, citing AI demand.

HP Inc rose 4.6% after beating quarterly revenue estimates on growing demand for AI-powered personal computers.

Packaging food company Hormel Foods tumbled 13% after issuing a downbeat quarterly profit forecast.

Declining stocks outnumbered rising ones within the S&P 500 (.AD.SPX) by a 1.2-to-one ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 28 new highs and five new lows; the Nasdaq recorded 117 new highs and 52 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was relatively light, with 13.8 billion shares traded, compared to an average of 16.7 billion shares over the previous 20 sessions.

data.title: New highs all around
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nasdaq ends up ~0.5%, S&P 500, Dow finish modestly green
  • Comm Svcs leads S&P sector gainers; Utilities weakest group
  • Dollar down; bitcoin dips; crude, gold gain
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield dips to ~4.21%

NEW HIGHS ALL AROUND

The main U.S. indexes gained on Thursday even after Nvidia's quarterly report fell short of investors' high expectations, while confirming that spending related to artificial intelligence infrastructure remains strong.

Shares of Nvidia ended down less than 1% on the day after Sino-U.S. trade uncertainties prompted the leading AI chip designer to exclude potential China sales from its quarterly forecast late on Wednesday.

In any event, the S&P 500 index ended above 6,500 for the first time, while scoring its 20th record close of 2025. The Nasdaq Composite notched its 21st record close of the year. The Dow registered its second record close so far this year.

It was the first time since December 4, 2024, that the three main indexes all scored a record close on the same day.

Among other notable record closes were the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF , the NYSE FANG+ index , the NYSE Composite , the Russell 1000 index , the S&P 100 index (.OEX), the S&P 1500 Composite index (.SPSUP), the S&P 500 Communication Services sector , the S&P 500 Financial sector , and the S&P 500 Banks Index (.SPXBK).

And, it may now actually not be unreasonable to consider the possibility that the lagging small-cap Russell 2000 index has the potential for new highs as well. It's now down 2.63% from its November 8, 2021 record finish.

Meanwhile, attention now turns to the release of July PCE inflation data before market open on Friday.

Expectations call for a month-over-month PCE index read of 0.2% vs 0.3% last month. The year-over-year print is expected at 2.6% vs 2.6% last month. The month-over-month core read is forecast to be 0.3% vs 0.3% last month, while the year-over-year core number is expected at 2.9% vs 2.8% last month.

data.title: Trump Fires Rail Regulator. Investors Are Taking Note. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

President Donald Trump's penchant for firing federal employees might have repercussions for railroad mergers. Investors are already responding.

On Wednesday, the president fired Surface Transportation Board member Robert Primus. The White House told The Wall Street Journal that "Robert Primus did not align with the President's America First agenda," adding "the Administration intends to nominate new, more qualified members to the Surface Transportation Board in short order."

Primus plans to challenge the firing, the Journal reported. "No cause was given," said Primus in a Thursday interview with CNBC, adding there was no prior conversation about job performance. "I have been pro-growth, pro-America since I've been [with the STB]." The two-sentence termination email came from the Office of Presidential Personnel.

The Surface Transportation Board is a federal agency that regulates railroads. It was created in 1996, replacing the Interstate Commerce Commission, which dated back to the 1800s, and has "jurisdiction over railroad rate, practice, and service issues and rail restructuring transactions, including mergers, line sales, line construction, and line abandonments."

The STB can have five board members. It currently lists four on its website; Primus is listed as a former board member. Primus, whom the Journal described as a Democrat, was the only member of the board to oppose the 2023 merger of Canadian Pacific and Kansas City Southern.

That is relevant today because railroad M&A, notoriously difficult to navigate past regulators, is heating up. In July, Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern announced plans to merge, creating a truly transcontinental railroad.

That deal was part of the reason activist investors Ancora recently urged CSX to look for a merger partner, suggesting either Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary BNSF or a Canadian railroad. Ancora portfolio manager Jim Chadwick told Barron's that CSX needs to act with more urgency, saying he believes the Trump administration would look favorably on mergers.

"I've said nothing publicly" about the merger," added Primus, while noting that in the past he has been "very concerned with further [railroad] consolidation." The potential for increased shipping costs and their impact on manufacturing from a more consolidated industry is something worth considering, he said.

Union Pacific stock was up slightly in late trading on Thursday, while Norfolk Southern shares gained 1.9%. The S&P 500 was up 0.4% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.2%.

Shares of CSX were up 0.2%, but stocks of the Canadian railways, seen as the potential buyers of the company, declined. Canadian Pacific Kansas City Southern stock was off 0.9% and Canadian National Railway shares fell 1.3%.

The Canadian railroads would likely need to pay a premium price for investors to agree to the sale, and shares of acquiring companies often fall in response to merger news. Investors may worry whether an acquirer will receive a high enough return to justify a lofty purchase price.

They may also seek to take advantage when an acquiring company uses stock to buy shares. That can create an arbitrage opportunity where investors buy the company being purchased and sell the acquirer's stock to lock in a spread.

Railroad mergers have worked in the past. Consolidation in the industry has led to stable prices and higher profit margins, but there hasn't been a major rail merger in more than 25 years. Kansas City Southern, acquired by Canadian Pacific, was a smaller railroad, and it had a special merger exemption from the STB.

It is still very early in the current railroad drama. Investors can count on more merger news in the coming months.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.description: "Lawmakers Press UnitedHealth on Hack Loan Repayments," at 2:07 p.m. ET, incorrectly attributed a quote from Sen. Ron Wyden to Sen. "After flunking cybersecurity 101, it's unacceptable for UnitedHealth Group to use hardball tactics to recoup loans to doctors left in a financial bedlam through no fault of their own," said Wyden. Warren described the reports of tight repayment demands as "deeply troubling" and urged UHG to provide flexible repayment schedules.
